description: SpyTag002 — second-generation 14-aa peptide tag (VPTIVMVDAYKRYK) that forms a spontaneous, irreversible isopeptide bond to SpyCatcher002, reacting ~12x faster than the original SpyTag.

references.authors: Keeble AH, Banerjee A, Ferla MP, Reddington SC, Khairil Anuar INA, Howarth M
references.title: Evolving Accelerated Amidation by SpyTag/SpyCatcher to Analyze Membrane Dynamics

references.authors: Zakeri B, Fierer JO, Celik E, Chittock EC, Schwarz-Linek U, Moy VT, Howarth M
references.title: Peptide tag forming a rapid covalent bond to a protein, through engineering a bacterial adhesin
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2012)

references.authors: Khairil Anuar INA, Banerjee A, Keeble AH, Carrington L, Bhatt D, Howarth M
references.title: Spy&Go purification of SpyTag-proteins using pseudo-SpyCatcher to access an oligomerization toolbox

references.authors: Keeble AH, Turkki P, Stokes S, Khairil Anuar INA, Rahikainen R, Hytonen VP, Howarth M
references.title: Approaching infinite affinity through engineering of peptide-protein interaction
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2019)

label: SpyTag002
note: Second-generation 14-aa peptide tag (VPTIVMVDAYKRYK) derived from the CnaB2 immunoglobulin-like domain of Streptococcus pyogenes fibronectin-binding protein FbaB, evolved from the original SpyTag (AHIVMVDAYKPTK) by phage-display selection plus rational design. SpyTag002 differs from SpyTag at 4/13 shared residues and carries one extra N-terminal residue. On mixing with its protein partner SpyCatcher002 it forms a spontaneous, irreversible intermolecular isopeptide (amide) bond (reactive Asp; partner Lys; catalysed by SpyCatcher Glu77); single Asp->Ala (DA) mutation abolishes the reaction. Fusable at the N-terminus or C-terminus. Backwards-compatible with the parental SpyCatcher.

provenance.sequence_source: ATN39889.1 (SpyTag002-MBP fusion, Keeble et al. 2017, Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. — PMID 29024296; SpyTag002 = residues 19–32, 100% identity over 14 aa, independently fetched and aligned)

functional_claims.label: Spontaneous, irreversible isopeptide (amide) bond to SpyCatcher002; second-order kinetics; DA mutation abolishes reaction

value.mechanism: intermolecular isopeptide (amide) bond formation with loss of water

source.quote: we confirmed the role of the putative reactive residues: single mutation in SpyTag002 (DA) or SpyCatcher002 (EQ) abolished reaction ... following second-order kinetics ... Loss of water upon SpyTag002/SpyCatcher002 reaction was confirmed by mass spectrometry
source.section: Characterization of spontaneous amidation between SpyCatcher002 and SpyTag002

functional_claims.label: Reacts with SpyCatcher002 at 2.0e4 M-1 s-1 (25 C, pH 7.0), ~12x faster than original SpyTag/SpyCatcher

value.format: measured as SpyTag002-MBP reacting with SpyCatcher002

source.quote: At 25 °C at pH 7.0, SpyTag002-MBP reacted with SpyCatcher002 with a rate constant of 2.0±0.2×10[4] M[-1]s[-1] (12 times faster than SpyTag-MBP reacting with SpyCatcher: 1.7±0.4×10[3] M[-1]s[-1]).

functional_claims.label: Reaction is robust across a wide pH and temperature range and tolerates detergents and >3 M urea

value.buffer_tolerance: relatively independent of buffer salts; tolerates common non-ionic detergents and over 3 M urea
value.completion: 99% (SpyCatcher002 with excess SpyTag002-MBP); 97% (SpyTag002-MBP with excess SpyCatcher002)

source.quote: SpyTag002 and SpyCatcher002 reacted under a wide range of pH and temperature conditions ... Reaction was relatively independent of buffer salts, tolerating common non-ionic detergents and over 3 urea. SpyCatcher002 reacted to 99 % completion with a small excess of SpyTag002-MBP. Conversely, SpyTag002-MBP reacted to 97 % completion with an excess of SpyCatcher002
source.section: Characterization of spontaneous amidation between SpyCatcher002 and SpyTag002

functional_claims.label: Fusable at either the N- or C-terminus

source.quote: SpyTag002 reacted efficiently when fused either to the N terminus as SpyTag002-MBP or to the C terminus as AffiEGFR-SpyTag002

functional_claims.label: Binds the non-reactive SpyCatcher variant SpyDock with Kd ~73 nM (ITC), ~10x tighter than original SpyTag

value.partner: SpyDock (SpyCatcher2.1 S49C E77A, a non-reactive SpyCatcher variant)

source.quote: we used isothermal titration calorimetry (ITC) to measure the dissociation constant for SpyDock's interaction with SpyTag-MBP or SpyTag002-MBP. Both proteins bound with 1:1 stoichiometry. SpyTag002-MBP (Kd = 0.073 μM) bound ~10-fold tighter than SpyTag-MBP (Kd = 0.75 μM)

functional_claims.label: Enables non-covalent affinity capture on immobilized SpyDock with imidazole elution (Spy&Go)

value.caveat: the tag must not have already reacted covalently with SpyCatcher/SpyCatcher002, which would render it unavailable for SpyDock binding

source.quote: A series of optimizations established that 2.5 M imidazole at neutral pH was efficient at eluting SpyTag from SpyDock, with lower imidazole concentrations used in the wash buffer.

functional_claims.label: Covalent module for live-cell surface labeling/imaging and for affinity purification of fusion proteins

applications: specific covalent pulse-labeling and fluorescence imaging of surface proteins on living cells
irreversible protein-protein conjugation as a faster protein superglue
affinity purification of tagged fusion proteins via SpyDock (Spy&Go)

source.quote: SpyTag002/SpyCatcher002 allowed specific covalent pulse-labeling of surface proteins on living cells and represents the fastest currently available Tag/Catcher pair.

functional_claims.label: Validated for recombinant fusions and for covalent labeling at the bacterial cell surface

validated_in: bacterial outer-membrane surface (intimin display in E. coli)

source.quote: SpyTag002 reaction enabled specific and covalent decoration of intimin for live cell fluorescent imaging of the dynamics of the bacterial outer membrane as cells divide.

functional_claims.label: Evolved from SpyTag by phage display plus rational design; differs at 4/13 residues with an extra N-terminal residue

value.parent_source: CnaB2 domain of Streptococcus pyogenes fibronectin-binding protein FbaB
value.engineering: phage-display selection (N- and C-terminal libraries) plus rational design; Cys reverted to Ala to avoid dimerization; terminal Lys added
value.differences_from_SpyTag: 4 of 13 shared residues changed plus one extra N-terminal residue (14 aa total)

source.quote: SpyTag002 has 4/13 residues that are different to those in SpyTag and an extra residue at the N-terminus.

functional_claims.label: Faster successor SpyTag003 (RG-extended) reacts 28x faster than SpyTag002

value.relationship_to_SpyTag002: N-terminal RG dipeptide appended plus further engineering

source.quote: 5.5 ± 0.6 × 10[5] M[-1]s[-1] (mean ± 1 SD, n = 3), ∼400-fold faster than the 1.4 × 10[3] M[-1]s[-1] previously shown for SpyTag/SpyCatcher, and 28-fold faster than the 2.0 × 10[4] M[-1]s[-1] for SpyTag002/SpyCatcher002.
